codeReview.summary: I reviewed the prior failed review, both feature handoffs, the fix transcript skeleton, both feature diffs (`764f697` and `66e2ad3`), and the current `lib/codegen.ml` / test coverage. The new recursive peeling-and-reentry logic in the four nested built-in match helpers addresses the round-6 blocker: deeper accepted nested Copy-payload `Option`/`Result` patterns now recurse instead of falling into generated panic branches, and default fallback continues to be threaded through the recursive calls. I found one non-blocking coverage/communication issue in the added ownership regression tests.

issues.description: The registered test name claims coverage for a deep `Result<Option<Result<T, E>>>` runtime case, but `test_own_deep_nested_result_option_copy_runtime` only exercises `Result<Option<i64>, str>` (`test/test_codegen_ownership.ml:1933-1956`). That overstates the added Result-depth coverage and can mislead future reviewers about which arbitrary-depth Result shapes are actually protected by runtime tests.

summary: Pass. The fix resolves the prior blocking issue from round 6: deeper accepted nested Copy-payload built-in patterns are now lowered recursively instead of reaching generated panic paths. I recorded one non-blocking issue about an overstated ownership test name, and no shared-state gaps were identified.
